Container.Name: MCSManager-Daemon
Container.Overview: MCSManager (MCSM) Daemon - the backend component that manages and runs your game server instances. Communicates with the MCSManager Web UI panel to launch, stop, and monitor servers. NOTE: This is the Daemon component. You must also run the MCSManager-Web container. After installation, open the Web UI, go to Nodes, set a name for the Node, and set the Daemon IP to YOUR_UNRAID_IP
Container.Requires: MCSManager-Web must also be running. But it's best to install this one first.

Config.Name: Daemon Port
Config.Description: Port used for communication between the Daemon and the Web UI panel.

Config.Name: Daemon Data
Config.Description: Stores daemon data including server instance files.

Config.Name: Minecraft Port Range
Config.Description: Port range for Minecraft server instances. Expand this range if you plan to run more than 10 servers.

Config.Name: Minecraft Port Range UDP
Config.Description: UDP port range for Minecraft Bedrock server instances.

Config.Name: Web Map Port
Config.Description: Optional port or port range for web map viewing (Bluemap, Dynmap) (eg. 8100, 8123)

Config.Name: Docker Workspace Path
Config.Description: Path where MCSManager stores game server instance data. Must match the Daemon Data path.

Config.Name: Docker Socket
Config.Description: Allows MCSManager Daemon to manage Docker containers for game servers.
